# Footguns

## observer-prompt.md remains after observer.js deletion

[Trigger]: When exploring the src/evolve/ directory or searching for files related to the observer subsystem. [Detail]: The observer.js module was deleted during subsystem deprecation, but observer-prompt.md was left behind orphaned with no code references. [Consequence]: Developers may assume the observer is partially active and attempt to modify observer-prompt.md to tune behavior, but changes have no effect because the subprocess is not running.

_Source: `src/evolve/observer-prompt.md`_

_Tags: `backend`_

## buildPlanPrompt truncates last file when final file exceeds cumulative maxKb, breaking prompt context

[Trigger]: When buildPlanPrompt() assembles candidate file paths and content for the plan suggestion context. [Detail]: The function iterates through candidate file paths accumulating content bytes. When the cumulative size would exceed maxBytes, the loop breaks and returns. However, if the LAST file's size alone exceeds the remaining budget, the loop breaks BEFORE checking if appending it would overflow, potentially returning a list where the last file's content size exceeds the maxKb limit. This was fixed by breaking BEFORE attempting to append when currentBytes + size > maxBytes. [Consequence]: Plan prompt context can silently exceed the configured maxKb limit, causing error_max_turns failures when invoked against Opus with --max-turns 1 constraint.

_Source: `src/review/plan.js`_

_Tags: `backend`, `error_pattern`_

## buildPlanPrompt truncation bug: last file can exceed maxKb limit

[Trigger]: When buildPlanPrompt() assembles candidate file paths and content for the plan suggestion context. [Detail]: The loop accumulates files until currentBytes + size > maxBytes, then breaks. However, this allows the last file added to exceed maxBytes if its size alone exceeds the remaining budget. The fix is to check BEFORE adding: if (currentBytes + size > maxBytes && result.length > 0) break. [Consequence]: Plan generation prompt can exceed intended size limits and trigger error_max_turns or E2BIG errors if the last file is very large (e.g., a 300KB rules.md file).

_Source: `src/review/plan.js`_

_Tags: `backend`_

## callClaude timeout insufficient for plan generation with large prompts

[Trigger]: When plan generation invokes callClaude() to synthesize implementation plans from daily review context. [Detail]: Plan generation passes handoff prompts reaching 500+ KB of daily review context (knowledge entries, project configs, work history). The default callClaude timeout of 60s is insufficient for Opus to process such large prompts. The callClaude wrapper in extract.js was extended to 120s via opts.timeout when invoked from plan generation. [Consequence]: Plan generation times out and fails silently if timeout is not extended, returning error_max_turns or ETIMEDOUT.

_Source: `src/knowledge/extract.js`_

_Tags: `backend`, `performance`_

## Plan generation cleans up orphaned running plans on server restart

[Trigger]: When the daily_reviews table contains plan_status='running' rows but the corresponding child process is not running (server crash or SIGTERM during plan generation). [Detail]: On createDb() during server startup, the schema migration flips any remaining plan_status='running' rows to 'error' with error message 'orphaned on server restart'. This prevents stale running status from persisting indefinitely and blocking UI polling. Without this cleanup, the UI would poll forever waiting for a plan that will never complete. [Consequence]: Orphaned running plans block the UI from retrying or accepting the suggestion until manually cleared via database.

_Source: `src/db/schema.js`_

_Tags: `database`, `backend`, `deployment`_

## Knowledge scan and extract pipelines have divergent effort tuning

[Trigger]: When quality issues arise in knowledge extraction or when comparing cold-start scan results to post-ingest extraction results. [Detail]: scan.js passes {effort: 'max'} for deeper reasoning, but extract.js does not pass effort to callClaude (defaults to standard reasoning). This creates an asymmetry where cold-start scans receive high-effort analysis but post-ingest extractions receive standard analysis. [Consequence]: Over time, knowledge base quality may degrade as new entries (post-ingest) lack the reasoning depth of bootstrap entries (cold-start), leading to inconsistent entry quality and missed patterns in ongoing sessions.

_Source: `src/knowledge/scan.js`_

_Tags: `backend`, `config`_

## buildPlanPrompt truncation fails to enforce maxKb limit on last file

[Trigger]: When buildPlanPrompt assembles candidate file paths and content for the plan suggestion prompt. [Detail]: The loop adds files until currentBytes + size > maxBytes, but does NOT check if the final result exceeds maxBytes — the last file can push total over limit. Test shows appending a 3rd file (1080 KB total) when limit is 1 KB. The fix: break BEFORE adding if currentBytes + size > maxBytes, not after. [Consequence]: Plan generation prompt exceeds maxKb limit unexpectedly, wasting tokens and potentially triggering Claude CLI argument size errors.

_Source: `src/review/plan.js`_

_Tags: `backend`_

## updatePlanStatus missing from exports but savePlan function uses related status column updates

[Trigger]: When persisting plan generation results to the daily_reviews table. [Detail]: The savePlan(db, id, planMd, handoffPrompt, runId) function performs the UPDATE directly rather than calling a separate updatePlanStatus() function. The daily_reviews table has a plan_status column that transitions from 'pending' (set at suggestion creation) to 'done' (set by savePlan). There is no exported updatePlanStatus function—status updates happen only within savePlan. [Consequence]: Trying to call updatePlanStatus() as a standalone operation will fail—plan status updates must happen via the full savePlan() function that also sets plan_md and handoff_prompt.

_Source: `src/review/queries.js`_

_Tags: `backend`, `database`_

## Ghost features render empty when API returns []

[Trigger]: When a backend function is deprecated and starts returning empty arrays, but the frontend card still renders. [Detail]: The Timeline card checked if data.length > 0 before rendering the chart, but if the check was missing, the card title and wrapper would render with no content — appearing as an empty card on the page. Users see a blank card taking up space, not realizing the feature is dead. The getProjectTimeline() function was changed to return [] after the cl_instincts table was dropped, causing the Timeline card to silently fail. [Consequence]: Empty cards waste screen real estate and confuse users; developers debugging the blank card spend time checking the frontend when the real issue is the backend returning no data.

_Source: `src/db/projects.js`_

_Tags: `frontend`, `backend`_

## Chart cleanup must be removed alongside UI code for dead features

[Trigger]: When removing a frontend component that renders Chart.js visualizations (e.g., Timeline). [Detail]: The projects.js module calls destroyCharts() at the start of renderDetail() to clean up previous chart instances. If the chart rendering code is deleted but destroyCharts() is not cleaned up, the function call remains as dead code that wastes lines and confuses reviewers. The Timeline feature removal found chart rendering code at lines 231-280 (timeline canvas, labels, datasets) that was completely removed. [Consequence]: Orphaned destroyCharts() calls indicate incomplete feature removal and pollute the codebase with dead references to removed variables.

_Source: `public/modules/projects.js`_

_Tags: `frontend`_
